bead.title: docs: reconcile doc stale review-hash governance
bead.description:
PROBLEM
`ddx doc stale --json | jq length` reports 138 stale docs, mostly because dependencies lack review hashes. That scale makes the stale signal too noisy to guide spec-first work.

ROOT CAUSE
The doc graph review-hash process is not being maintained for many active HELIX artifacts, or `ddx doc stale` is over-reporting missing hashes without enough operational triage. Sample output includes core FEAT, ADR, API, and TD documents such as API-001 and FEAT-010.

PROPOSED FIX
Reconcile the staleness workflow: decide whether to stamp/review the active graph, suppress/scope historical artifacts, or improve `ddx doc stale` output so missing review hashes produce actionable buckets. The result should leave operators with a small, meaningful stale set or a documented recovery process.

NON-SCOPE
Do not mechanically stamp every doc without review. Do not rewrite unrelated HELIX content except to mark historical/superseded artifacts if that is the chosen policy.
bead.acceptance:
1. `ddx doc stale --json` output is triaged into active-actionable vs historical/superseded/noise buckets, either by docs updates or CLI behavior.
2. At least one regression test or documented command proves active docs with missing dependency review hashes are reported in an actionable way.
3. Historical artifacts are either excluded from the active stale count or explicitly marked so their staleness does not obscure active work.
4. `ddx doc stale --json | jq length` is lower than the current 138 count, or a documented rationale explains why the count remains high but actionable.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"ambiguous_scope: The bead is a multi-scope governance bundle, not a single implementation task. It asks to choose among policy triage, historical-artifact suppression, or CLI output changes, while the current doc-graph code already has a concrete missing-review-hash reason path (`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:884-898`) and FEAT-007 already frames `ddx doc stale` plus a phased migration (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-007-doc-graph.md:240-246`). The missing decision is which behavior the work is supposed to codify.","fingerprint":"65d16f07edfea00b621ddaa951d728b509d4064639c033166b45b9dfcf38f181","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"65d16f07edfea00b621ddaa951d728b509d4064639c033166b45b9dfcf38f181"}
